pi_items.source: Windows environment configuration shows user 'Docker' exists; path structure is consistent with Windows conventions, but file operations cannot be verified without Windows environment access
Direct HTTP HEAD request to https://api.vitaldb.net/0001.vital and file download confirmation. File is gzip-compressed (~21MB) and uncompressed original is ~77MB.

WebFetch and WebSearch confirmed https://vitaldb.net is the official VitalDB website. Nature Scientific Data publication confirms dataset. PhysioNet hosting confirmed at https://physionet.org/content/vitaldb/1.0.0/

VitalDB documentation and case file download confirms case IDs range from 1-6,388. Case 1 is accessible at https://api.vitaldb.net/0001.vital. Downloaded and verified as valid gzip-compressed vital file.

pi_items.verified_value: VitalDB Browser is NOT documented as a feature within Vital Recorder. Downloads occur via vitaldb.net website, API, or VitalSync tool.
pi_items.source: Exhaustive search of VitalDB documentation, Vital Recorder manuals, and technical papers. No mention of 'VitalDB Browser' as a Vital Recorder feature. The Vital Recorder User Manual documents recording features, not case downloading. File download guide at vitaldb.net documents web browser interface, VitalSync, and API methods—none integrated into Vital Recorder itself.

privileged_info_summary: VitalDB is a legitimate, freely accessible open dataset at https://vitaldb.net containing 6,388 surgical cases with high-resolution vital signs. Case 1 exists and is accessible, with actual file size of 21,063,820 bytes (approximately 20.1 MB compressed, ~77 MB uncompressed), significantly exceeding the 1 MB minimum requirement. However, the claimed 'VitalDB Browser' feature with integrated case selection and downloading functionality does not appear to exist as part of Vital Recorder; case downloads happen through the VitalDB website interface, API, or VitalSync tool instead.
